statements.statement: I can identify that people vote to choose leaders in our community.
I can describe how elections help people choose who will make decisions for them.
I can explain how voting works and identify different types of government roles.
I can describe how democratic elections lead to government formation and basic government functions.
I can explain the relationships between elections, government formation, and different levels of government.
I can analyse how democratic processes connect elections to government structures and their specific roles.
I can evaluate the effectiveness of democratic governance structures in different political and social contexts.
I can justify how democratic systems balance power through elections and multi-level governance across diverse societies.

I can justify how democratic governance structures balance power distribution between different governmental institutions and citizens.
I can critically analyse democratic governance effectiveness across diverse political contexts and constitutional frameworks.

I can justify how different democratic systems and government structures respond to complex societal challenges and changing contexts.
I can identify simple ways to help in my classroom and school community.
I can identify different ways to participate in school decisions and local community activities.
I can identify various opportunities to contribute to community groups and local decision-making processes.
I can identify and describe multiple pathways for civic participation in local and regional governance.

I can identify and evaluate civic participation opportunities considering their impact on democratic processes and outcomes.
I can identify and justify strategic civic participation approaches for addressing complex societal challenges effectively.

I can identify that laws are rules that help people live together safely.
I can explain that laws exist to keep people safe and describe simple rules.
I can describe the main purposes of laws and identify basic types of laws.
I can explain how laws protect people and property while identifying different categories of laws.
I can analyse the purposes of laws and compare different types of laws systematically.
I can evaluate the effectiveness of laws in achieving their purposes across various legal contexts.
I can justify the purposes of laws and analyse relationships between different types of laws.
I can critically evaluate how laws serve multiple purposes and apply legal frameworks across contexts.

I can identify that courts, judges, and police help keep people safe in our community.

I can evaluate the effectiveness of cooperation between courts, judges, and law enforcement in different justice scenarios.
I can critically analyse how courts, judges, and law enforcement interactions impact justice outcomes across diverse contexts.

I can identify that Ancient Greek ideas helped create New Zealand's voting system with teacher support.
I can describe simple connections between Ancient Greek democracy and New Zealand's democratic practices.
I can explain how specific Ancient Greek political concepts influenced New Zealand's democratic development.
I can compare Ancient Greek democratic principles with New Zealand's current democratic system and institutions.
I can analyse the transmission of Ancient Greek political ideas through history to New Zealand's democracy.
I can evaluate the significance of Ancient Greek influence on New Zealand's democratic evolution and contemporary governance.
I can critically analyse how Ancient Greek political philosophy shaped New Zealand's democratic framework across multiple contexts.
I can synthesise complex relationships between Ancient Greek political theory and New Zealand's democratic development independently.
I can identify different sources of information about community issues with teacher support.
I can recognise that people have different opinions about the same civic topic.
I can compare two different viewpoints on a local community issue using guided questions.
I can evaluate the reliability of information sources when researching a civic decision.
I can analyse multiple perspectives on civic issues and explain their underlying reasoning.
I can critically assess bias in information sources and justify which viewpoints are most credible.
I can synthesise complex civic information from diverse sources to form well-reasoned personal positions.
I can evaluate competing civic arguments across contexts and construct sophisticated evidence-based recommendations.
